phase_ready.note: Resolves at PHASE-READY: PH-1.2 (P1), PH-1.3 (P1), PH-2.3 (P2), PH-2.4-EMPIRICAL (P2), P10-GAP-2.5 (P2). 9 outbound C4-tracking edges to P4-P8, P9, MK-P4.

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: P1 D-1.1 — T_char = 4*tau_gen derived from Hopf quarter-wave theorem. Live edge. Not stipulated.
P1 F-1.4 + P2 C-2.1 — beta=1/2 derived analytically from Hopf quarter-wave theorem and RG fixed-point argument. Not fitted. Live edges.
IR-13 — standard goodness-of-fit anchor. Bevington and Robinson 2003 §4.1. Not stipulated by FRM.
C-3.sigma Step R6 + Bevington and Robinson 2003 §3.2 — substrate-specific sigma derived from regression residuals, not stipulated. Resolves P2 P10-GAP-2.5.
PH-3.VAC-1 resolution — T_window = 3*T_char (three full periods). Derived from P1 D-1.1 T_char expression and measurement resolution requirement. Not stipulated.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: FRM System Definition
claim_registry.statement: A system S is an FRM-scope system iff: (a) S exhibits sustained or transiently-decaying oscillation; (b) the oscillation is generated by delayed negative feedback with a single dominant delay tau_gen > 0; (c) tau_gen is independently measurable (i.e. measurable by methods not derived from the FRM period prediction T = 4*tau_gen). Condition (c) is the independence requirement and is the FRM solution to the DQ problem.
claim_registry.note: Type D — definitional claim. Not subject to falsification predicate. Gate: any system cited in P3 validation must satisfy all three conditions.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: tau_gen Extraction Protocol
claim_registry.statement: tau_gen is extracted from a system S by the following procedure: Step 1 — identify the biological or physical mechanism producing delayed negative feedback. Step 2 — measure the delay of that mechanism using methods independent of the oscillation period T. Step 3 — record tau_gen with units and measurement method. Step 4 — confirm tau_gen > 0. The extracted tau_gen is the sole parameter input to the FRM prediction T = 4*tau_gen. No fitting is performed.
claim_registry.note: Type D — protocol definition. Alpha sub-rule (HR-3.1): alpha is a system property, not a protocol parameter. Default alpha = -1 is the dimensionless normalisation for sub-critical systems (P1 scope boundary). Literature lookup is preferred source for alpha. See Section 3 prose note.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: FRM Regression Protocol — Primary Validation
claim_registry.statement: When the FRM functional form f(t) = B + A*exp(-lambda*t)*cos(omega*t + phi) is fitted to a time-series from a D-3.1-satisfying system using the 9-step regression protocol R1-R9, with tau_gen extracted per D-3.2 and all parameters derived (not fitted), the FRM model achieves R2_frm >= 0.85 and R2_frm > R2_best_alt.

WHERE.R2_frm_below_threshold: BOOLEAN — TRUE if R2_frm < 0.85 for any D-3.1-satisfying system in the validation set. R2_frm: coefficient of determination from 9-step protocol R1-R9 applied to FRM functional form with zero free parameters.
WHERE.best_alt_model_superior: BOOLEAN — TRUE if R2_best_alt > R2_frm for any system in the validation set. R2_best_alt: R2 from best-fitting alternative model (C-3.ALT protocol). Alternative models: exponential decay, sinusoidal without decay, polynomial trend.
WHERE.validation_set: All systems in P1 Appendix A satisfying D-3.1(a)(b)(c). n = confirmed substrates per P1 F-1.2.
WHERE.R2_frm: SCALAR — coefficient of determination from protocol R1-R9 step outputs. Computed as 1 - SS_res/SS_tot.
WHERE.R2_best_alt: SCALAR — maximum R2 across alternative models evaluated per C-3.ALT protocol.

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Step 1: for each system in validation_set, apply D-3.2 to extract tau_gen. Step 2: compute derived parameters (lambda = |alpha|/(Gamma*tau_gen), omega = pi/(2*tau_gen), T_char = 4*tau_gen). Step 3: apply 9-step protocol R1-R9. Step 4: compute R2_frm. Step 5: apply C-3.ALT to compute R2_best_alt. Step 6: evaluate both Boolean conditions. Step 7: if either TRUE, verdict = FALSIFIED.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Gamma = 1 + pi^2/4 DERIVED (P1 F-1.4 live edge). tau_gen independently measured (D-3.1(c)). alpha from literature or default -1 (P1 scope boundary). No fitted parameters. Threshold R2 >= 0.85 anchored per IR-13 (Bevington and Robinson 2003 §4.1). T_window >= 3*T_char per PH-3.VAC-1.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-3.REG is the primary validation claim for P3. Resolves PH-1.2 and PH-1.3 in P1 AI layer v11. Resolves PH-2.3 and PH-2.4-EMPIRICAL in P2 AI layer v4. Provides measurement standard for P4-P8. Formal ancestor of kappa_corpus in P9. Cross-track: provides tau_gen extraction for MK-P4 FRM-governed substrates.

claim_registry.section_4_note: T_char vs T_obs distinction (HR-2.1): T_char = 4*tau_gen is the FRM theoretical prediction. T_obs is the measured period from the time series. These are NOT the same quantity. The validation tests whether T_obs approximates T_char — it does not use T_obs as an input to the FRM. This distinction must be stated explicitly in Section 4 prose.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Alternative Model Comparison Protocol
claim_registry.statement: The FRM functional form systematically outperforms the three canonical alternative models (exponential decay only, sinusoidal without decay envelope, polynomial trend) on all D-3.1-satisfying systems in the validation set, as measured by R2 comparison under the C-3.ALT protocol.

WHERE.any_alt_model_superior_on_any_system: BOOLEAN — TRUE if for any D-3.1-satisfying system in the validation set, any alternative model achieves R2_alt > R2_frm. Alternative models: (1) pure exponential B + A*exp(-lambda*t); (2) pure sinusoidal B + A*cos(omega*t + phi); (3) polynomial B + c1*t + c2*t^2.
WHERE.R2_alt: SCALAR — maximum R2 across three alternative models, fitted with free parameters for fair comparison

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each system in validation_set: (1) fit three alternative models with free parameters; (2) compute R2_alt for each; (3) take maximum; (4) compare against R2_frm from C-3.REG. Verdict FALSIFIED if max R2_alt > R2_frm for any system.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Alternative models fitted with free parameters to ensure comparison favours alternatives. FRM uses zero free parameters. If FRM loses to a model with free parameters, the FRM is falsified. Systems failing D-3.1 are not counterexamples.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-3.ALT provides the comparative benchmark for C-3.REG. The comparison is deliberately unfair to the FRM (alternatives have free parameters). This makes the test more stringent, not less.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Diagnostic Protocol — Residuals and Scope
claim_registry.statement: For any D-3.1-satisfying system where C-3.REG produces R2_frm < 0.85, the diagnostic protocol C-3.DIAG identifies a specific violation of one of the D-3.1 conditions (a)(b)(c) as the cause. Systems failing C-3.REG without a diagnosable D-3.1 violation constitute a falsifying instance for the FRM scope definition.

WHERE.unexplained_failure: BOOLEAN — TRUE if any system satisfies D-3.1(a)(b)(c) AND produces R2_frm < 0.85 AND no D-3.1 condition violation can be identified as the cause of failure.
WHERE.diagnosable_violations: List of accepted diagnostic categories: (1) tau_gen measurement error — D-3.1(c) violated; (2) multiple competing delays — D-3.1(b) violated; (3) non-sustained oscillation (noise floor) — D-3.1(a) violated; (4) T_obs/T_char discrepancy exceeding 20% — measurement protocol error, not FRM failure.

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each system with R2_frm < 0.85: (1) check residual structure for systematic pattern; (2) test each D-3.1 condition independently; (3) if a condition violation is identified, record cause and classify as out-of-scope; (4) if no violation identified, verdict = FALSIFIED.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Diagnostic protocol does not rescue failed predictions — it classifies them. A system correctly identified as D-3.1-violating is not a counterexample to the FRM. A system that satisfies D-3.1 and fails C-3.REG with no diagnostic explanation IS a counterexample.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-3.DIAG is the scope-enforcement protocol. It prevents the FRM from being unfalsifiable by excluding inconvenient data — it instead makes explicit the boundary conditions under which failure is expected.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Substrate-Specific Sigma Protocol
claim_registry.statement: For each substrate class in the validation set, the substrate-specific measurement uncertainty sigma_S is derived from the regression residuals of the C-3.REG protocol using the Bevington-Robinson residual variance method. sigma_S is not stipulated. The 2-sigma bound in C-2.4 (substrate independence) is computed from this derived sigma_S, not from an assumed value.

WHERE.sigma_S_not_derivable: BOOLEAN — TRUE if for any substrate class, the regression residuals from R1-R9 do not yield a finite, positive sigma_S via the Bevington-Robinson method.
WHERE.residuals_non_normal_AND_sigma_invalid: BOOLEAN — TRUE if Kolmogorov-Smirnov test rejects normality of residuals at p < 0.01 AND the Bevington-Robinson sigma estimate is shown to be invalid under non-normal residuals for that substrate class.

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each substrate class: (1) collect residuals from C-3.REG R1-R9 output; (2) apply Bevington and Robinson 2003 §3.2 variance formula; (3) compute sigma_S; (4) test residual normality (KS test); (5) if normality rejected, apply bootstrap sigma estimate as fallback; (6) record sigma_S with method.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Resolves P2 P10-GAP-2.5. sigma_S derivation path: C-3.sigma Step R6 + Bevington and Robinson 2003 §3.2. Method is anchored to published statistical authority (IR-13), not stipulated by FRM authors. Fallback: bootstrap method if normality rejected.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-3.sigma closes the final open Principle 10 gap from P2 (P10-GAP-2.5). Substrate-specific sigma replaces the P1 F-1.3 sigma for P2 C-2.4 evaluation, making the 2-sigma bound derived rather than inherited.

regression_protocol.name: P3 Nine-Step Regression Protocol
regression_protocol.ir_compliance: IR-12 — each step has named input, deterministic operation, named output
regression_protocol.statistical_anchor: IR-13 — Bevington and Robinson 2003 as primary statistical authority
regression_protocol.zero_free_parameter_constraint: SATISFIED — all FRM parameters derived from tau_gen and IR rules. No fitting of FRM parameters.

steps.operation: Apply D-3.1 scope check — verify conditions (a)(b)(c)
steps.output: PASS/FAIL scope verdict. FAIL exits protocol.

steps.operation: Apply D-3.2 tau_gen extraction — measure delay by substrate-appropriate independent method

Determine alpha — look up bifurcation distance from literature. If unavailable, use default alpha = -1 (dimensionless sub-critical normalisation). Alpha sub-rule: alpha is a system property, not a protocol parameter.
Compute derived parameters: lambda = |alpha|/(Gamma*tau_gen), omega = pi/(2*tau_gen), T_char = 4*tau_gen
Set observation window T_window >= 3*T_char. Extract time series segment.

steps.input: Time series segment, derived parameters lambda, omega
steps.operation: Fit FRM form f(t) = B + A*exp(-lambda*t)*cos(omega*t + phi) with B, A, phi as the only fitted parameters (3 free parameters — offset, amplitude, phase). lambda and omega are NOT fitted.
Compute sigma_S per C-3.sigma protocol (Bevington-Robinson §3.2). KS normality test.
Fit three alternative models with all parameters free: (1) B + A*exp(-lambda*t); (2) B + A*cos(omega*t + phi); (3) B + c1*t + c2*t^2. Compute R2 for each.
steps.output: R2_best_alt = max of three alternative R2 values

steps.operation: Evaluate C-3.REG predicate: (R2_frm >= 0.85) AND (R2_frm > R2_best_alt). Evaluate C-3.ALT predicate: R2_frm > R2_best_alt.
steps.output: PASS or FAIL verdict with supporting values

placeholder_register.description: Vacuity witness for C-3.REG — minimum observation window T_window.
placeholder_register.resolution: RESOLVED Phase 3 — T_window = 3*T_char = 12*tau_gen. Derived from D-1.1 (T_char = 4*tau_gen) and measurement resolution requirement (three full periods minimum). Not stipulated. Documented in R5.

placeholder_register.description: Vacuity witness for C-3.DIAG — diagnostic categories must be non-exhaustive (protocol must be capable of producing unexplained_failure=true).
placeholder_register.resolution: RESOLVED Phase 3 — diagnostic categories (1)-(4) do not exhaust all possible failure modes. A system exhibiting oscillation with no identifiable feedback delay and no D-3.1 condition violation would produce unexplained_failure=true. Protocol is non-vacuous.

hr_resolution_log.resolution: T_char = 4*tau_gen is theoretical prediction. T_obs is measured period. These are not the same. Validation tests whether T_obs approximates T_char. Documented in C-3.REG section_4_note and R4.

hr_resolution_log.challenge: alpha default value — is it stipulated?
hr_resolution_log.resolution: alpha = -1 is the dimensionless normalisation for sub-critical systems at P1 scope boundary. It is a system property, not a protocol parameter. Literature lookup preferred. Default documented in R3 alpha sub-rule.

hr_resolution_log.challenge: Vacuity witness for C-3.REG — is T_window derived or stipulated?
hr_resolution_log.resolution: T_window = 3*T_char = 12*tau_gen. Derived from D-1.1 and measurement resolution requirement (three full periods). Not stipulated. Documented in PH-3.VAC-1 and R5.
